title: Multi-level items board issue
excerpt: Even though the multi-level item board is cool, there’s a limitation. I want to have seperate columns for my parent item and my sub items. I’d like the parent item to be a high level overview of the task, and then go int…

posts.author: Samss.o (Samira)
posts.cookedHtml: Even though the multi-level item board is cool, there’s a limitation. I want to have seperate columns for my parent item and my sub items. I’d like the parent item to be a high level overview of the task, and then go into detail in the sub items (hence have different columns). For normal boards, we were able to have this, but for some reason in the multi-level board it isn’t there. There should be an option on whether you want to copy parent columns or change it

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey, not sure I understand fully, is this the new functionality just released with multi levels of subitems? Perhaps it would need two views to achieve what your looking a top level and full sub level view? Can you still use the mirror column roll up to get an idea progress etc?

posts.author: warthvader (Stephen Warth)
posts.cookedHtml: Samira - 100% Agree! - How did they mess this up so bad! - Since you are locked into the same columns as the parent (or level 0) there is NO benefit at all to this type of “sub” items. Might as well just start a new row.. is the SAME THING! how did this “feature” take 6 years develop? We NEED the traditional subitems (that allows for an entirely unique set of columns on Level 1 and then be able to have a Level 2,3,4 etc. Continually beyond disappointed in this product, support and the nickel and diming.

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello all, Tom from monday here I completely see your point, just here to remind everyone that the feature is still in beta, you can still expect improvements moving forward We are totally aware of the need for different columns for each level, I really hope we’ll be able to add that capability! I took the liberty to move this discussion to the Feature Request page, in order to give you and all the community members the ability to vote this up.

posts.author: Jbengston (Jaime Bengston)
posts.cookedHtml: This! I was so excited for it… we finally got access to it, and its basically pointless the way its set up. I am so disappointed.

posts.author: abaker (Alexa)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that we need separate columns for each level - Furthermore we need the ability to ‘set’ a rollup from a specific level. Ie: The ability to say in column {Y} roll up from level 4 only, in column {X} rollup only from level 3 (even if a level 4 exists), and in Column {Z} rollup level 2 and level 4 but dont roll up level 3.

posts.author: Paul_Adams (Paul Adams)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree 100% - the idea is perfect to bring together different work flows, but these work flows require different sub item columns structure from the main items. In its current form I’m not sure what the use case is? Any timeline for changing this?
